subject: DevOps
title: Container Image Scanning
description: A quick win: understand Container Image Scanning.

hook.text: The image used ubuntu:latest. It had 247 known CVEs. Nobody scanned it before deploying to prod.

buildup.text: Container images bundle OS libs, runtimes, and app code. Any layer can contain known vulnerabilities.

discovery.text: Trivy, Grype, and Snyk compare image contents against vulnerability databases and report findings.

twist.text: Use minimal base images. Alpine or distroless images have fewer packages and smaller attack surfaces.

climax.text: Scan in CI/CD and block images with critical CVEs. Scan registries for newly disclosed flaws too.

punchline.text: Scan every image before deploy. Smaller bases mean fewer flaws.

quiz.question: Why are minimal base images like Alpine recommended for containers?
options: They contain fewer packages, reducing the attack surface
